_meta.methodology: BUILD_PROCESS.md (14-param + principles + functional/security/concurrency tests); P0.7 zero-debt

title: Lead Import (CR-B): staged upload->validate->commit workflow, large-import dual-approval gate, XLSX support
business_reason: CR-A imported synchronously in one call. import_policy.large_import_requires_approval_threshold_rows = 5000 requires an approval gate, and the canonical lead_import_lifecycle defines staged statuses (uploaded -> validated -> imported). CR-B implements the staged batch workflow with a dual-approval (maker != checker) gate for large imports, and adds XLSX upload alongside CSV.

decisions_resolved.flow: Always staged (two-step): every import = validate -> commit, regardless of size (user choice).
decisions_resolved.xlsx: Added now: the `xlsx` (SheetJS) dependency parses .xlsx client-side to the same row shape as CSV.
decisions_resolved.row_persistence: Pending importable rows are held server-side in school_lead_import_batches.validation_report.pending_rows (jsonb) so a different approver can commit without re-uploading. (A dedicated staged-rows column/file storage is a future optimization; reusing the jsonb avoids a migration, P0.5.)

param_14.question: Stage imports through validate->commit, gate large imports (>=5000) behind maker!=checker approval, and accept XLSX.
param_14.answer: importLeads becomes stage 1 (validate + dedupe + create a 'validated' batch holding pending_rows; NO insert). commitImport is stage 2 (loads the batch; if total_rows>=5000 records dual-approval and only inserts at distinct>=2; else inserts on first commit) via the shared buildLeadRow, with per-row errors captured. cancelImport (reason required) -> cancelled. Routes: POST /import (stage 1), /import/[batchId]/commit, /import/[batchId]/cancel. UI: file -> Validate -> batch preview -> Commit/Cancel; XLSX parsed via SheetJS dynamic import.
param_14.data_entities: school_lead_import_batches (status transitions + pending_rows), school_leads (insert at commit), approvals, audit_events
param_14.workflow_entities: lead_import_lifecycle: upload_import -> validated; commit_import -> imported/partially_imported (guard large_import_approval_if_required); cancel_import -> cancelled
param_14.security_implications: commit is staff-write; >=5000 requires two DISTINCT approvers (P3.4, recordApproval) — same actor twice cannot self-approve. pending_rows/reports are restricted jsonb. created_by/lead_code/stage/lead_status server-set via buildLeadRow (P3.9). cancel requires a reason (P1.8); audited (P3.5).
param_14.approval_implications: large_import_requires_approval_threshold_rows=5000 enforced at commit; held at 'validated' until a second approver commits.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: reuses approvals.recordApproval (kernel dual-approval). XLSX adds the `xlsx` npm dependency.
param_14.assumptions: Approval threshold compares total_rows. Re-validation of a batch is a fresh upload (no edit-in-place of a staged batch in CR-B).

principles_pass.staged_workflow: P3.10/P3.3 — validate->commit honors lead_import_lifecycle from-states; commit only from 'validated'
principles_pass.dual_approval: P3.4 — >=5000 needs maker!=checker; returns applied:false until distinct>=2; reuses recordApproval
principles_pass.reuse: P0.6/P0.5 — buildLeadRow shared; recordApproval reused; no new approval mechanism, no migration
principles_pass.silent_failure: P4.5/P0.7 — per-row insert errors captured; nothing reported imported that wasn't
principles_pass.reason_required: P1.8 — cancel requires a reason; audited

functional: stage 1 stages a 'validated' batch and inserts NO leads
commit a small batch inserts full server-set leads -> imported
security_owasp: A03: required-column validation; verbatim cell storage
A08: CSV server fields ignored at commit
dual-approval: same approver twice cannot self-approve; second distinct approver applies
concurrency: two parallel stage-1 uploads -> distinct batch_code
maker!=checker race resolves to a single apply

verification: tests/unit/crm_import.test.ts (16 tests). 118/118 pass, tsc clean (incl. xlsx).

known_followups.dedupe_perf: findDuplicates (server/crm/dedupe.ts) is O(n^2); a 5000-row validate takes ~6s. Acceptable for now but should move to hash-keyed dedupe (by normalized_name+city+state / email / phone / website) for large imports. Tracked — not a correctness bug.
known_followups.staged_rows_store: pending_rows in validation_report jsonb is pragmatic; a dedicated staged store or persisted source_file (Supabase storage) is the cleaner long-term home.
known_followups.xlsx_audit: added `xlsx` (SheetJS) dependency; npm audit shows pre-existing advisories in the tree (not introduced by this change) — review separately.
